description: Create the apps/admin Next.js 15 application with full project scaffolding, navigation layout, dashboard page, and all admin panel pages (apps, users, services, AI, security, database, settings). This is the complete admin panel implementation as a single coherent feature.
steps: Step 1 - Project scaffold: Create apps/admin/ with package.json (name: @quant/admin, deps: next ^15.5.16, react ^19, react-dom ^19, @quant/shared-ui workspace:*, @quant/common workspace:*, @quant/database workspace:*, @quant/server-core workspace:*, @quant/auth workspace:*, @tanstack/react-query ^5.60.0, zod ^3.24.2, framer-motion ^11. devDeps: @types/react ^19, @types/react-dom ^19, tailwindcss ^3.4.13, postcss ^8, autoprefixer ^10, vitest ^2). Scripts: dev, build, start, typecheck, test, lint same pattern as apps/quantmail.

Step 2 - Config files: Create next.config.js (transpilePackages: ['@quant/shared-ui', '@quant/common'], output: 'standalone'), tsconfig.json (same pattern as apps/quantmail/tsconfig.json with paths for @quant/shared-ui and @quant/common), turbo.json (extends root, build outputs .next/**), tailwind.config.ts (content includes src/ and ../../packages/shared-ui/src/**, extends with quant brand colors), postcss.config.js (tailwindcss + autoprefixer), vitest.config.ts (node environment, backend tests).

Step 3 - Global styles and layout: Create src/app/globals.css (same CSS variables and Tailwind setup as quantmail). Create src/app/layout.tsx with metadata (title: 'Admin Panel | Quant Ecosystem'), Inter font, dark mode support, QueryProvider and AuthProvider wrappers.
Step 4 - Providers: Create src/providers/query-provider.tsx (same pattern as quantmail). Create src/providers/auth-provider.tsx with admin-only auth context that checks user role === 'ADMIN'.

Step 5 - Admin layout with sidebar: Create src/components/AdminLayout.tsx - a responsive sidebar layout with: Quant logo at top, navigation links (Dashboard, Apps, Users, Services, AI/ML, Security, Database, Settings), user profile section at bottom, collapsible on mobile. Use Tailwind classes matching the quant design system.

Step 6 - Dashboard page (src/app/page.tsx): Overview page showing: system stats cards (Total Users, Active Apps, Requests/min, Error Rate), a grid of all 16 apps with health status indicators (green/yellow/red circles), quick action buttons per app. Use mock data initially with proper TypeScript types. Import from @quant/shared-ui where possible (Card, Badge, Button, Skeleton).

Step 7 - Apps Management page (src/app/apps/page.tsx): List all 16 ecosystem apps in a table/grid. Each app shows: name, status (enabled/disabled toggle UI), version, port, last deploy time. Include a search/filter bar. Link each app name to a detail view.

Step 8 - Users Management page (src/app/users/page.tsx): User table with columns: avatar, name, email, role (Admin/Mod/User badge), status, joined date, actions (ban/suspend/verify dropdown). Include pagination, search, role filter. Use @quant/shared-ui components.

Step 9 - Services Monitor page (src/app/services/page.tsx): Show all 8 infrastructure services (ws-gateway, smtp-inbound, cdc-relay, ci-runner, git-server, matchmaking, moderation-worker, search-indexer). Each service card shows: name, status dot, uptime %, last restart, memory/CPU usage bars, recent log entries section.

Step 10 - AI/ML Dashboard page (src/app/ai/page.tsx): Show AI provider statuses, model usage stats (tokens used, requests, avg latency), cost tracking cards, content moderation queue count. Include charts placeholder areas for usage over time.

Step 11 - Security Center page (src/app/security/page.tsx): Recent auth events table (login attempts, failures), rate limiting stats, active sessions count, suspicious activity alerts list. Security score card at top.

Step 12 - Database page (src/app/database/page.tsx): Show connection pool stats, migration status (latest migration name + pending count), table overview (name, row count, size), slow queries section.
Step 13 - Settings page (src/app/settings/page.tsx): Feature flags section with toggles, notification settings form, API keys management (masked display), ecosystem configuration form fields.

Step 14 - API route for health aggregation: Create src/app/api/health/route.ts that defines the health check endpoint pattern. It should export async GET handler that returns aggregated health status for all apps (stubbed with realistic mock data showing all 16 apps and 8 services).
Step 15 - API route for stats: Create src/app/api/stats/route.ts returning ecosystem-wide statistics (user counts, request rates, error rates) from mock data with proper types.
Step 16 - Ensure the admin app builds: The package.json, tsconfig, next.config, and all pages must be valid TypeScript that compiles with `next build`. No import errors, no missing modules.
acceptance_criteria: apps/admin/ directory exists with all config files (package.json, next.config.js, tsconfig.json, turbo.json, tailwind.config.ts, postcss.config.js, vitest.config.ts)
src/app/layout.tsx provides admin shell with sidebar navigation
All pages use TypeScript with proper types - no `any` in page components
Components import from @quant/shared-ui where applicable (Card, Badge, Button, etc.)
Design uses Tailwind with quant CSS variables for consistent styling
verification: pnpm install (to link the new workspace package)
findings: Build and typecheck pass. All 8 pages + 2 API routes + layout + providers created. Pages that import from @quant/shared-ui need 'use client' directive because the shared-ui barrel export re-exports components using React hooks. Prettier reformatted files via lint-staged on commit. next-env.d.ts was auto-generated by the build.
